summary: Iteration 2 — software-agency pivot + admin CMS. Backend: 15/15 pytest tests pass (health, content, leads, auth, admin protected). Frontend: public landing renders all 9 sections (Hero, LogoMarquee, HowItWorks, Features, Industries, Testimonials, FAQ, CTA, Footer); NO pricing section anywhere; hero copy matches agency framing ('We turn your business idea into shipping software', 'Software Solutions Studio'); CTA form happy-path and validation work. Admin login (good + bad creds), JWT in localStorage 'plg_token', all 10 admin tabs render, hero-eyebrow edit -> save -> public reflects -> revert works, industries add/remove works, leads list + delete works, logout clears token, /admin protection redirect works, /nope 404 page renders with data-testid='404-home-link'.

minor.issue: Returns 200 instead of 201 on creation; convention is 201 for POST create. Functional but inconsistent.
limit query uses raw HTTPException(400) for out-of-range. FastAPI Query(ge=1, le=1000) would return 422 and document the constraint.

issues: Field selectors are 'admin-login-email' / 'admin-login-password' but original spec mentioned generic 'admin-email-input' style. Either is fine, but worth confirming docs. (No functional issue.)

action_items: (Optional) Return 201 for POST /api/leads and POST /api/status; align with REST convention.
(Optional) Convert admin-leads `limit` to Query(ge=1, le=1000) so OpenAPI docs and 422 responses are consistent.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py — admin auth implemented cleanly with HTTPBearer + JWT (HS256) and idempotent admin seed (also rotates hash if password env changed). Good.

PUT /api/admin/content replaces the document fully via $set on payload; if a client omits a key, the previous value is preserved (because $set only updates provided fields). This is safe but mildly surprising — consider documenting that PATCH-semantics are in effect, or implement a real replace. Not a bug.
No rate-limit/brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (not required by spec, but a good hardening note for a CMS endpoint).
JWT_SECRET is committed to .env (env file is local-only). Fine for preview.
Frontend AdminDashboard.jsx has admin-save & admin-tab-* and ind-items-add/remove-N test ids — all align with spec.
Footer test id is 'site-footer' (not 'footer') and process section is 'how-it-works-section' (not 'process-section'). Spec used generic names; tests confirm functional sections exist.
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_*@example.com leads via POST /api/leads during pytest and one TEST_pw_iter2@example.com lead via the public CTA form. Most TEST leads were cleaned up by the admin DELETE flow; a few may remain in db.leads.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend health, content, auth and admin endpoints all green. AdminLogin test ids are 'admin-login-email', 'admin-login-password', 'admin-login-submit', 'admin-login-error'. AdminDashboard uses 'admin-tab-<key>' for tabs, 'admin-save' to persist, 'admin-logout' to clear token, 'ind-items-add' / 'ind-items-remove-N' for arrays, and 'lead-delete-{id}' to remove a lead. JWT is in localStorage key 'plg_token'. The Hero section uses 'hero-section', process uses 'how-it-works-section', footer uses 'site-footer'. CTA submit button is 'cta-submit-button'. No pricing UI anywhere. Hero copy: 'We turn your business idea into shipping software' + eyebrow 'Software Solutions Studio'.
